info.name: crypto-gateway
info.description:
Complete flow coverage for crypto-gateway: admin bootstrap, merchant API (invoices + payouts), public checkout, provider webhook ingest, and internal cron.

## Quick start
1. Import this collection AND `crypto-gateway.local.postman_environment.json` (Environments tab).
2. Select the `crypto-gateway (local)` environment.
3. Fill in `adminKey` in the environment. Other values are auto-populated by test scripts as you run requests.
4. Run `_Setup` → `1. Health` to confirm connectivity.
5. Run `_Setup` → `2. Create merchant` — its test script saves `apiKey`, `merchantId`, `webhookSecret` into the environment for subsequent merchant API calls.
6. Use the per-chain quick-starts under `Invoices / Quick starts` to create sample invoices on each supported chain.

## Chaining
Test scripts write `merchantId`, `merchantApiKey`, `webhookSecret`, `invoiceId`, `invoiceReceiveAddress`, `payoutId`, and per-chain `alchemySigningKey_<chainId>` into the environment so later requests can consume them without copy-paste.

## Webhook simulation
The `Webhooks (simulate)` folder fires synthetic Alchemy payloads at `/webhooks/alchemy`. The pre-request script computes the correct HMAC using `alchemySigningKey` from the environment, so after bootstrap + signing-key save, the ingest route accepts them as if from Alchemy.

## Server-side config (not used by Postman — reference only)
These env vars affect the gateway's behavior but aren't exercised by any request in this collection. Documented here so operators testing against a deployed gateway know what knobs exist.

**Price oracle fallback chain.** Invoice quoting walks a chained list of providers. Each link tries itself first; on error/timeout/unmapped symbol it delegates. Only the terminal `static-peg` link serves hardcoded values. Default ordering with `ALCHEMY_API_KEY` set: `CoinGecko → Alchemy → CoinCap → Binance → static-peg`.
- `PRICE_ADAPTER` — `coingecko` (default), `alchemy` (Alchemy-first), or `static-peg` (skip all live sources)
- `COINGECKO_API_KEY`, `COINGECKO_PLAN=demo|pro`, `COINCAP_API_KEY` — optional, raise the per-provider rate budget
- `DISABLE_COINGECKO=1`, `DISABLE_COINCAP=1`, `DISABLE_BINANCE=1`, `DISABLE_ALCHEMY=1` — drop individual providers from the chain

**Log shipping + alerting.**
- `ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL` — `error`/`fatal` logs are POSTed here as JSON. Sliding-window rate-limited to 30/min, with `droppedSinceLast` reported on the next delivery
- `ALERT_WEBHOOK_AUTH_HEADER` — value for the `Authorization` header (e.g. `Bearer xyz`)

See the repository README for the full env-var table.

exec: // Fresh, collision-resistant externalId per request so Runner loops
// Collection-level sanity: a 5xx is always a bug worth flagging.

item.name: _Setup
item.description: Bootstrap flow. Run top-to-bottom in Collection Runner to get a working merchant + API key + (optionally) Alchemy webhook registration.

request.description:
Creates a merchant and returns the plaintext `apiKey` (saved into env) + plaintext `webhookSecret` (saved into env). Neither is recoverable afterward — Postman stores them in the environment so downstream requests chain automatically.

`paymentToleranceUnderBps` / `paymentToleranceOverBps` set the merchant's default invoice payment tolerance in basis points (1 bp = 0.01 %; capped at 2000). `0 / 0` is strict — every cent must match. Per-invoice overrides on `POST /api/v1/invoices` win when set. Tweak via `PATCH /admin/merchants/:id` later.

Updates the merchant's payment-tolerance defaults (basis points, 1 bp = 0.01 %).

- `paymentToleranceUnderBps`: a paid USD amount within `bps` of the target closes the invoice as `confirmed` instead of `partial`. 50 = 0.5 %.
- `paymentToleranceOverBps`: a paid USD amount within `bps` over the target closes as `confirmed` instead of `overpaid`. 100 = 1 %.

Applies to invoices created **after** this PATCH; existing invoices keep the snapshot they were issued with. Webhook URL/secret are intentionally NOT mutable here — changing them mid-flight orphans pending outbox rows enqueued under the old key.

Seeds the address pool. Creates N HD-derived addresses per family (evm/tron/solana), marks them 'available', fires `pool.address.created` events that the Alchemy subscription tracker fans out to per-chain webhook registrations.

Idempotent: re-running tops up families below target size. Families whose chain adapter isn't wired on the gateway are reported as `skipped-no-adapter`.

Subsequent invoice creations allocate from this pool. When the available count drops below the refill threshold, a background refill fires automatically — but the initial seed is explicit so operators see the pool filling up before traffic arrives.

Per-family pool snapshot: available / allocated / quarantined / total + highest address_index. Watch this with an alert on available-count under threshold to catch low-supply before POOL_EXHAUSTED fires on a live invoice-create.

Registers a fee wallet for payouts. The gateway HD-derives the `(address, privateKey)` pair from `MASTER_SEED` at a deterministic index keyed by `(family, label)` and returns the derived address in `feeWallet.address`. No private key is accepted here — the operator funds the returned address out-of-band. The same `label` always derives to the same address across redeploys. Use `chainId: 999` + `family: evm` for local runs; for real chains use the appropriate chainId from the table in the README.

item.name: 4b. Manually register a signing key (dashboard-created webhook)

request.description:
Only needed when you created a webhook directly in the Alchemy dashboard UI (skipping bootstrap) OR when a bootstrap run returned `persisted: false` on a `created` row. Encrypts the signingKey via `SECRETS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` and upserts into `alchemy_webhook_registry`. Re-running with the same `chainId` rotates (dashboard delete+recreate workflow).

Most operators should NOT need this — bootstrap persists signing keys automatically.

Idempotently creates one ADDRESS_ACTIVITY webhook per chain on Alchemy, pointing at `$GATEWAY_PUBLIC_URL/webhooks/alchemy`. The webhook URL is NOT accepted in the body — it's built from the `GATEWAY_PUBLIC_URL` env var on the gateway (security: prevents a leaked ADMIN_KEY from redirecting Alchemy's traffic).

Per-chain signing keys are persisted encrypted in `alchemy_webhook_registry`. The test script also saves the first `created` key into `alchemySigningKey` so the webhook simulator can HMAC-sign payloads.

**Gateway env requirements:**
- `ALCHEMY_NOTIFY_TOKEN` — **the webhook management token**, not the JSON-RPC API key. Get it from the top of https://dashboard.alchemy.com/apps/latest/webhooks (labelled 'Auth Token'). Using `ALCHEMY_API_KEY` here returns `401 AuthError` from Alchemy. (Old name `ALCHEMY_AUTH_TOKEN` still accepted with a deprecation warning.)
- `GATEWAY_PUBLIC_URL` — public origin of this gateway (e.g. `https://your-worker.workers.dev`).
- `ADMIN_KEY` — set on the gateway AND in this Postman environment.

exec: // Per-chain env var so a multi-chain bootstrap doesn't collapse keys.
// Also populate the generic `alchemySigningKey` with the first created key
// so single-chain flows + the webhook simulator have a default.

item.name: Invoices
item.description: Merchant-scoped invoice API. Requires `merchantApiKey` (auto-populated by `_Setup / 2. Create merchant`).

request.description:
Uses `chainId`, `token`, `amountRaw` from the environment. Flip chains by changing the environment values; or use one of the `Quick starts` requests that pre-set them.

Alternative shape: omit `amountRaw` and provide `fiatAmount` + `fiatCurrency` to let the gateway quote the crypto amount against the price oracle.

Optional per-invoice webhook override: pass `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret` together to route this invoice's events to a URL/secret different from the merchant-account default. Both must be supplied as a pair (one without the other is a 400). The URL is echoed back in the response; the secret is stored encrypted at rest and never returned. Dispatch precedence: invoice override → merchant default → skip. See `Create invoice (with per-invoice webhook)` for an example.

Same as the parametric Create invoice, but sets a per-invoice `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret`. Events for THIS invoice (`invoice.detected`, `invoice.confirmed`, etc.) dispatch to the per-invoice URL signed with the per-invoice secret instead of the merchant-account default. Response echoes `webhookUrl`; `webhookSecret` is never returned.

Per-invoice payment tolerance override. `paymentToleranceUnderBps: 50` (0.5 %) means a 1.99 USD payment against a 2.00 USD invoice closes as `confirmed` instead of `partial`. `paymentToleranceOverBps: 100` (1 %) means a 2.02 USD payment closes as `confirmed` instead of `overpaid`. Each is capped at 2000 bps (20 %); omit either to inherit the merchant default. Snapshotted at create time — a later `PATCH /admin/merchants/:id` does not retroactively reshape this invoice.

Transitions the invoice to `expired`. Idempotent-ish: already-terminal invoices return 200 with their current state. Frees the HD-derived receive address for reuse lifecycle-wise (Alchemy address-subscription sweep will deregister it).

item.name: Quick starts
item.description: Pre-filled `Create invoice` variants — one per supported chain × stablecoin. Each overrides its own `chainId`/`token`/`amountRaw` via request-scoped variables so you don't have to edit the environment to try a different chain. Each saves `invoiceId` + `invoiceReceiveAddress` on success.

request.description: BSC USDT is **18 decimals** (Binance-Peg BEP-20, not the 6-decimal native tokens on other chains). 1 USDT = 10^18 raw. Don't hardcode 6 anywhere.
Requires `TRONGRID_API_KEY` on the gateway for incoming-transfer detection. Payouts work with Alchemy alone; detection does not.
SPL USDC on Solana mainnet. Receive-only today — detection via Alchemy ADDRESS_ACTIVITY webhook (parses `event.transaction[].meta.post_token_balances`). SPL payouts are deferred.
Local-only test chain. Payouts work against a synthetic broadcast shim — perfect for integration testing without touching a real network.

item.name: Multi-family — USDT accepted on EVM + Tron + Solana

request.description:
Multi-family acceptance (A1.b). Invoice is allocated one pool address PER family; payer picks which chain to pay on and the gateway detects wherever it lands.

Response includes `receiveAddresses: [{family, address}, ...]` — render all three at checkout. `chainId` + `receiveAddress` echo the primary family (first in the list) for legacy clients.

Requires the pool to be seeded for every family in `acceptedFamilies` (see `_Setup > 3a. Initialize address pool`). Missing chain adapters → `TOKEN_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

item.name: Multi-family — USDT accepted on EVM + Tron only

request.description: Subset of families. Two receive addresses returned — payer can pay USDT on any of the 7 EVM chains OR on Tron mainnet. Same invoice satisfies whichever chain gets the payment first.

item.name: Universal USD invoice ($25, all families, any token)

request.description:
USD-pegged invoice payable from ANY supported chain in ANY token the gateway can price. Difference vs. `Multi-family — USDT`:

- `amountUsd` instead of `amountRaw` — invoice tracks the $25 target across whichever token/chain pays.
- Rates are pinned at creation for 10 minutes (the rate window). Pay with USDC on Polygon, ETH on Ethereum, USDT on Tron, SOL on Solana — all convert via the pinned snapshot.
- `chainId` + `token` are the *primary* family (used for the legacy `receiveAddress`). Real acceptance is everything in `acceptedFamilies`.

Response contains `acceptedFamilies`, `receiveAddresses[]`, `amountUsd`, `paidUsd`, `rates`, and `rateWindowExpiresAt`. Follow up with `Get invoice` to see the live USD breakdown + per-tx detail (including any reverted/detected rows).

The checkout page (`GET /checkout/:id`) renders one row per (chain, token) combination via the `payableTokens` field — pre-computed minimum amounts ready to pay.

Alternative invoice-creation shape: instead of `amountRaw`, pass `fiatAmount` + `fiatCurrency`. The gateway quotes the token amount via `deps.priceOracle` (default = static USD peg for stablecoins) and persists the quoted rate on the invoice.

item.name: Payouts
item.description: Merchant-scoped payout API. Requires a registered fee wallet on the target chain (see `_Setup / 3. Register fee wallet`).

request.description:
Creates a `planned` payout. The `executeReservedPayouts` cron job picks it up, CAS-reserves a fee wallet, builds + signs + broadcasts the transfer. Polls `getConfirmationStatus` each tick until finalized.

Optional per-payout webhook override: pass `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret` together to route this payout's events (`payout.submitted`, `payout.confirmed`, `payout.failed`) to a URL/secret different from the merchant-account default. Both must be supplied as a pair (one without the other is a 400). The URL is echoed back in the response; the secret is stored encrypted at rest and never returned. Dispatch precedence: payout override → merchant default → skip. See `Plan payout (with per-payout webhook)` for an example.

Same as the parametric Plan payout, but sets a per-payout `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret`. Events for THIS payout dispatch to the per-payout URL signed with the per-payout secret instead of the merchant-account default. Response echoes `webhookUrl`; `webhookSecret` is never returned.

item.name: Checkout (public)
item.description: Unauthenticated checkout view — the URL your frontend or Telegram bot renders for the payer.

request.description: Returns the invoice in a payer-safe shape: receive address, required amount, expiry, status. `merchantId`, `metadata`, `externalId` are intentionally NOT exposed to the public path.

item.name: Webhooks (simulate provider)
item.description:
Fake Alchemy webhook payloads sent through the real ingest route, signed with the HMAC key saved by `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks` (or set manually via `alchemySigningKey` in the environment).

The pre-request script computes `HMAC-SHA256(body, signingKey)` hex and puts it in `X-Alchemy-Signature` — same flow the real Alchemy service uses.

exec: // Sign the raw body with the Alchemy signing key and put the hex HMAC into X-Alchemy-Signature.
if (!key) { throw new Error('alchemySigningKey not set — run `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks` or set it manually in the environment'); }

request.description: Simulates an incoming USDC SPL transfer (1.000000 USDC) to the Solana receive address saved by the most recent 'Create invoice' against Solana. Payload shape matches Alchemy's protobuf-JSON serialization (1-element arrays for `transaction` / `message` / `meta`).

exec: if (!key) { throw new Error('alchemySigningKey[_900] not set — run `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks` with chainIds including 900, or set `alchemySigningKey_900` manually'); }

item.name: Admin / Webhook deliveries
item.description: Operator-only dead-letter queue for outbound webhook deliveries. Use these when a merchant webhook has been failing and you've coordinated a fix.

request.description: Returns a page of webhook delivery rows filtered by status. Default `status=dead` surfaces rows that exhausted the retry schedule (5 outer attempts × up to 4 inline HTTP attempts each) and require operator intervention before the merchant sees the event.

Resets a row from `dead` to `pending` with `next_attempt_at = now`, so the next scheduled-jobs sweep re-dispatches it. Returns 404 with code `NOT_DEAD` if the row is not in dead status — pending rows are already queued and delivered rows don't need replay.

Set `deadDeliveryId` from the List-dead-deliveries test script, or paste a specific id.

item.name: Internal
item.description: Endpoints the gateway operator hits directly — not the merchant surface, not public.

request.description:
Runs the full scheduled-jobs sequence out of band: `pollPayments → confirmTransactions → executeReservedPayouts → confirmPayouts → sweepStuckFeeWalletReservations (→ alchemySyncAddresses)`.

Outside Cloudflare Workers / Deno Deploy (which have native cron), this is the only way to advance state between invoices. Vercel Cron hits this on a schedule; for local Node runs it's handy to invoke manually after creating an invoice and simulating a webhook.

Returns `{ ok: true, result: { <perJobOutcome> } }`.
